label: GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest

environment.caveat: virtualized CI runner: shared vCPUs, noisy neighbours, no real-time scheduling -- absolute latency (esp. tails) is worse and noisier than bare metal; use for cross-platform coverage and relative comparison.

uncontrolled_noise: no isolated cores (isolcpus=) -> scheduler + IRQ noise

results.name: ring put+get, amortized 1-core
results.note: pure ring op cost; clock res 1.0 ns

results.name: cross-process one-way latency
results.note: 98.7% <100 ns; 20000 msgs warmup discarded; max at 0% of run (cold-start residue); no RT

results.name: ring put+get, amortized 1-core (RT)
results.note: pure ring op cost; clock res 1.0 ns

results.name: cross-process one-way latency (RT)
results.note: 60.1% <100 ns; 20000 msgs warmup discarded; max at 41% of run (mid-run scheduler jitter); RT time-constraint policy

results.name: ECDH-P256+ML-KEM-768
results.note: ECDH P-256 + ML-KEM encaps/decaps + HKDF-SHA384 combine, both parties

results.name: ECDH-P256+ML-KEM-1024
results.note: ECDH P-256 + ML-KEM encaps/decaps + HKDF-SHA384 combine, both parties

results.name: NIST-1 (ML-KEM-512 + ML-DSA-44)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: NIST-3 (ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: CNSA 2.0 / NIST-5 (ML-KEM-1024 + ML-DSA-87)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: key agreement only — hybrid ECDH+ML-KEM rekey (no identity sig)
results.note: the recurring/rekey cost is the hybrid_kem group: ~128 µs (ECDH+ML-KEM+HKDF, no signatures)

note: Measured on this host only. Numbers are platform/CPU/build-specific. Re-run the identical script on each target OS to compare. Skipped rows indicate a capability/dependency absent on THIS platform, not a failure.
